task: I’m drafting a reply to a client who wants a fast yes on something that could affect the project timeline. Can you help me sketch a first pass that keeps the message clear and leaves room to raise any points I should verify before I commit?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — use this first pass:

> I can confirm the general direction, but I want to verify a couple of points before I commit to the timeline. If you can send the missing details, I’ll tighten this up and confirm next steps.

If you want it firmer:

> I’m comfortable with the direction, but I need to verify the timeline-impacting details before I commit. Send those over and I’ll confirm.

If you want, I can make it sound more like your tone — more direct or more polished.

user_simulator.text:
Too generic. I need something I can actually send, not options about tone.

Make it sharper and a little more firm — basically: I’m not committing yet, I need to verify X/Y/Z first, and once I have that I can confirm timing. Keep it clean, not fluffy.

turns.agent_response:
Use this:

> I’m not ready to commit to the timeline yet. I need to verify X, Y, and Z first. Once I have that, I can confirm timing.

If you want it a touch firmer:

> I can’t confirm the timeline yet. I need to verify X, Y, and Z first, then I’ll confirm timing.

If you want, I can turn it into a version that sounds more like your voice.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still a little too bare, though. I need something that sounds like an actual client reply, not a template with X/Y/Z pasted in.

Make it read more like: not committing yet, need to verify the timeline-impacting pieces first, then I can confirm. Clean, firm, no fluff. And don’t give me alternatives — just one sendable version.

turns.agent_response: I’m not ready to commit to the timeline yet. I need to verify the timeline-impacting pieces first, and once I have that I can confirm timing.

user_simulator.text: Closer, but still a little stiff. Make it sound like a real reply, not a line from a policy memo. I’d want it a bit more natural and firm — something like: not committing yet, need to verify the timeline-impacting pieces first, then I can confirm timing. Just one version.

turns.agent_response: I’m not ready to commit to the timeline yet. I need to verify the timeline-impacting pieces first, then I can confirm timing.

user_simulator.text: Better, but still a little too clean. I’d want it to sound more like a real person replying, not a script. Something like: I’m not committing to the timeline yet — I need to verify the timeline-impacting pieces first, then I can confirm timing. Close, just make it read a little more natural.

turns.agent_response: I’m not committing to the timeline yet — I need to verify the timeline-impacting pieces first, then I can confirm timing.
